raw_consumer_statement:
Hello, I am filing a complaint against Citibank , N.A . for improperly denying my Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) dispute and reversing my provisional credit based on incorrect and contradictory merchant evidence. 

This dispute involves an XXXX reservation ( confirmation code XXXX ) for a stay on XXXX XXXX XXXX  The reservation was booked, modified, and canceled within XXXX hours for a trip more than XXXX months away. XXXX applied a cancellation penalty of {$450.00}. 

Citi denied my dispute after relying on a XXXX cancellation policy supplied by XXXX. However, XXXX own system currently shows a materially different cancellation policy attached to this exact reservation, allowing partial refunds until XX/XX/year> XXXX days before check-in. XXXX also changed the full-refund window from XXXX hours to XXXX hours after booking, creating multiple conflicting policy versions. 

Because the merchants documentation is contradictory and XXXX failed to preserve a single, fixed set of terms, Citi could not legally enforce the stricter version. Under the FCBA, contradictory merchant evidence and misleading disclosure constitute a billing error that must be resolved in favor of the consumer. 

Despite receiving documentation showing these inconsistencies, Citi closed the dispute and reversed my provisional credit. 

Citibank violated my rights under the Fair Credit Billing Act by : Enforcing a cancellation policy that XXXX itself no longer attaches to the reservation Accepting contradictory merchant documentation without reconciliation Ignoring misleading and ambiguous cancellation disclosures Closing the dispute despite clear evidence of misrepresentation The cancellation terms were not reliably disclosed, not preserved, and not consistently applied.
